content:
Sunfall(20%) + Nymph SF(10%) = 18% poison diff

In arc1 sunfall work like need. But in arc2 it's only 18% more poison. But it must be 30% more poison
It's must be more then 30 no?

testing in dtide wave 3, with chains unbound +12 and sunfall +12 

with chains wheel - 281 poison 
with sunfall - 332 poison 

this is an increase of 18.1% with sunfall 

i’m pretty sure this value should be higher then 20% since sunfall gives 20% base damage and 20% poison

Sunfall + wolf 9,28% diff vs only sunfall 
Sunfall + wolf vs dmg amp 25,87 diff 
Sunfall vs dmg amp 16,68% diff  

All tests on arc2 rules. but where 10 more % from SF???
Bonuses within the same multiplier zone are ‌cumulative‌, not multiplicative, can it explain?
that makes sense
though does that mean it has diminishing returns ?
For 100 base damage, no matter how many times you add 20%, you get an extra 20 damage each time. If you consider this diminishing returns, then it is.
but the 20% base damage seems not to be accounted
yes because poison is a debuff, not damage
dont all exalt / cards with flat poison number have a base value ?
and base damage% should work fine on "Midnight Tide", where +Poison% is not work
base value of stack, not damage<:Brown_think:1459080117308620996>
? but both nymph exalt and midnight tide is att x %
oh right
deal damage
this zone is also contains dmg amp ? are they in same bracket
if i remember right, they are in 2 different zone
yes they should be different
just trying to confirm
yeah it's better to confirm your self, it's hard to get from the text
yeah too many things
what i know is "wielder's Base DMG%" from the WOD "Drowning in Crimson", is different from "Base DMG of the wielder's cards" from the WOD "Chains Unbound"
i assume drowning is retrospective with gelot’s rouse scaling ? while chains take default base damage ?
So, if understand right (1+10%(SF) + 20%(sunfall) + 10%(steppenwolf) ) / (1+10%) = 1.4/1.1 =1,2727 <:please:1356053110375714817> What a scam <:takenotes:1357186646294138890>
I guess it should be drowning +40% is adding to Gelot's rouse +15%, and the result multiplies with Chains +40%
weird, i thought base damage % adds on top of it self
it sounds right, about 30% increase
anyway i think you've got the idea, and it is being hard for me to express in English, so <:dead:1356082627509092372>
Yea, thx to explain
Have a good day/evening/night

yeah i understand. i think the reason why they are multiplied is because they are different base damage types 

gelot rouse and drowning wod is general base damage, while chains is base damage specific to cards 

so both of them will multiply each other
yes yes👍
ty :)

<@1485258210209435701> Can you(dev team) can change description on the sunfall to make it more clarify?<:please:1356053110375714817>  I mean now "Base DMG, poison and counter +20%" looks like wielder DEAL more poison and counter but he don't. Add word about it please "Base DMG, fixed poison and counter +20%" <:please:1356053110375714817>

content:
I just wanna know what decision prevented them from just making it a selector banner. you could even keep the "daily" thing if that's so important, by just making it so you can change banners once a day.
Then split wheels and characters like *literally every gacha does* and it's fixed. This stuff isn't hard to figure out. It's not even like that would decrease spending or anything. The monetisation philosophy of this game continues to elude me, it seems increasingly like the devs are afraid of making too much money on accident or something

Yesss And some say these banners are for making money... like what whales are gonna spend regardless ,if they wanted a charcter they'll go for oe or aa ,they dont care about rates.

If the banners were good and sepeate as many suggested more dolphins/less spenders would pay to get specific charater to oe or wheels to e3 etc ,since they only want specific things like only dupes or wheels they wont spend money now with these banners.

Point if my logic is wrong guys but imho if the banners are good many low spenders would have bought packs to pull on rerun banners,resulting in more money.(i have seen many say they thought of buying packs but decided not to after seeing these scam banners)

*Did these type rerun banners worked really great that they bought   them again? If its true then my opinion can go to hell im wrong and all power to devs i guess
Afaik, many people are pulling on Daily banners and Kaleido despite them being subpoptimal, cause of fomo and lack of options. So it kinda works for Devs i guess

I don't think it's for money. Basically nothing about this game is particularly well monetised, especially for a gacha game, and this current fiasco is just another instance of that.

That being said, it's not like the packs have worse value just because the banner is bad
Kaleidos are limited only, which is really nice for accounts only just starting out

Let's be real, 95% of players aren't interested in getting their units E12, most won't even go to OE, so for those people (Like me) the Kaleido banners are honestly, *fine*. They have the nice addon of not having to consider the "what if next rerun banner has better units" question, because this one has *all* of them and you can plan out your future pulls based on which ones you get here. I got G Murphy and Corposant, and I like Corposant a lot so I'll try to get a team that's fun with her (admittedly, Aurita is all she actually needs). I really don't mind the Kaleido banners for that reason
the daily banner though, is terrible *specifically* because it does still allow for standard characters

if you had a slim chance of getting a *different* limited character, most medium accounts and small time spenders would go "oh well, I'll take that chance". But if there's a chance of standard characters it's a whole other thing
I mean if people are spending to pull on those banner i guess the fomo works but also we got 100 pulls and new players get ton from stories so
standards are so easy to come by normally that although they're actually useful, getting spooked is still *really frustrating*
actual gacha newbies don't spend most of the time
it's probably largely people in my general situation but without the necessary insight/prior experience to look in the banner terms first
Also 100 pulls is like a drop in the ocean in this game Imma be honest
I did more than 1000 pulls in my first month of playing, full f2p

Yep most of the pulls we see are form anni free pulls and  honeymoon phase rewards.

Again if they spend money and fomo banners really work thats good too ,they can be staple and we have to expect them every anni .
Yes starting rewards are great in this game (or like any gacha game )

Yeah I'm not going to lie, I did pull on the Kaleido because for me everything was kind of an upgrade anyway, it was either getting new units because I was still lacking 3 of them (and got 2 of them so that was extremely lucky), or upgrading those I already had. I have now some char close to OE4 that I would like to focus on their next rerun, and got some wheel upgraded too so I won't complain. I would've liked to focus some of them if we had the daily banner rotating like the 2nd anniversary tho, like for example with the Kaleido my Vortice went from E3 to OE2, being that close to OE4 I would 100% go for it if it was available

btw, regarding the monetisation thing I said earlier. 

One other thing that's just odd about that in this game is, that, not including the first purchase bonus, buying silver for 50$ gives you 3280, and 100$ gets you 6480. Anybody capable of doing basic maths will realise that buying silver for 50$ twice is better than for 100 once. That's the opposite of how it usually goes. 
It is also the only outlier tbf the others all give more silver/$ the more you spend

oh, also purchases are made in home currency based on current exchange rate to US dollars, so if you live in a place with relatively unstable currency you may get more or less silver for your money depending on the day
but this has nothing to do with the rerun banners anymore, sorry for the tangent everyone
